text:
Once upon a time, there was a small town nestled between a sprawling forest and a tranquil lake. The townsfolk lived simple lives, working the land and enjoying their leisure time by the water.

Every year, there would be a festival celebrating the town's unique ability to turn pebbles into stars. It was a magical event, remembered fondly all through the seasons.

One year, though, the stars missed the festival. While the townsfolk tried everything, no magic could turn ordinary pebbles into shining celestial bodies. Panic spread quickly.

The wise old mayor convened a town meeting. "Friends," he began, "we've lost our talent. Something must change."

The villagers gathered, brainstorming wildly. Ideas flew around like leaves in autumn. Yet none seemed to work. A quiet girl named Lily approached.

"Maybe," she said softly, "the stars just need a new kind of magic."
